title: The Seven-Figure Deal Feels Natural
subtitle: A visualisation for experiencing large-scale investment as normal and within reach
excerpt: A short visualisation for the Day 75 theme of anchoring trust in multi-million investments. The scene is a negotiation or a signing where the numbers are large and your internal state is calm. The point is not the drama of the size but the ordinariness of the competence.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Forward projection imagery of this kind appears across coaching and therapeutic traditions. No single source is credited.

practice.whenToUse: Use when multi-million investment feels like something that belongs to a different category of person, and you want to rehearse it as normal.

content.text: A short visualisation for the Day 75 theme of anchoring trust in multi-million investments. The scene is a negotiation or signing where the numbers are large and your internal state is calm. The point is the ordinariness of the competence, not the size of the number.
Sit somewhere quiet. Close your eyes or soften your gaze. Two or three slow breaths.

You are looking at a number. It could be on a screen, a term sheet, a valuation. The number is large. Seven figures. You have seen numbers like this before in this visualisation and you will see them again. For now you are just looking at it.

Notice your breathing. It is steady. Your hands are still. You are not racing through the calculation — you have already done the due diligence, talked to the right people, looked at the numbers from multiple angles. What remains now is a decision, and you know how to make decisions.

You say something. It is practical. It is the next question or the next step. The other people in the room receive it as ordinary. You are doing the thing people do when they operate at this level. The only thing that has changed is that you are one of those people now.
Let the scene sit for a moment. The number on the document. The steadiness in the room. Then open your eyes.
Original to homemade.education. Forward projection imagery appears across therapeutic and coaching traditions — no single lineage is claimed.
